description:
# Open question

States as extensible config. Defaults: `[open, claimed]` (NO `review` — the review verb was dropped, §9); terminal ops are `close`/`drop` (not states). Per §4, `states` is informational only.

Pre-answered by note 0 (do not re-open): `state` is an OPEN string field — balls does NOT validate a transition matrix. The only hardcoded rule is `claim` refusing an already-claimed ball; all other sequence enforcement is a plugin `<op>/pre/` check (destination semantics, not source). So custom states "just work" — `[open, ready, in-progress, review]` needs no balls change.

Residual questions:
- `bl claim`'s default target state is `claimed`. If a user defines intermediate states, should claim's target be configurable per-op (e.g. `claim` → `in-progress`)? Or is the target purely a config default the op reads?
- `bl new --state ready` to override the create-state, vs initial state purely from config (`default_state`)?
- Worked example: a plugin enforcing "review → closed only when CI is green" hooks `close/pre/`.
title: Topic: states as extensible config
